summary: Refactoring verification completed. All 22 backend tests passed. Auth routes (routes/auth.py), Admin routes (routes/admin.py), and Payment routes (routes/payments.py) all work correctly after being extracted from server.py. server.py reduced from ~8000 to ~6260 lines with modular routers using set_db pattern.
critical_code_review_comments: Code review: Refactoring is clean and follows good patterns. Each modular router (auth.py, admin.py, payments.py) uses set_db() pattern for database injection which is correct.
The routes/auth.py properly excludes password_hash from responses using Pydantic Field(exclude=True)
Admin routes correctly check is_admin_email() for authorization
Payment routes properly handle plan mapping and user lookup
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactoring verification complete. All modular routes work correctly: (1) Auth routes in routes/auth.py: login, register, forgot-password, reset-password, verify-email, get user by ID. (2) Admin routes in routes/admin.py: users list, db-status with admin_email authorization. (3) Payment routes in routes/payments.py: plan features, user plan info. All endpoints return correct status codes and data structures. The set_db() pattern is used consistently across all modular routers.

endpoints_tested: POST /api/auth/login - returns user data without password_hash
GET /api/plan/features - returns 5 plans (free, explorer, learner, achiever, master)
